title: Rꜣ nw prt m hrw (125)
titleEn: Book of the Dead of Ani — Spell 125: the Negative Confession (all 42 declarations)
source: Papyrus of Ani (British Museum EA 10470), Book of the Dead Spell 125B — the 42 declarations of innocence addressed one by one to the assessor gods. Hieroglyphs transcribed from E. A. W. Budge, The Papyrus of Ani (1895), interlinear text, Plates XXXI-XXXII (pp. 198-203); Budge's transliteration and English gloss consulted as a meaning-check. Transliteration in the Leiden Unified convention; translation made fresh from the original grammar. Shown left-to-right; the papyrus runs right-to-left. Pronunciation convention: conventional Egyptological (langCode null, no TTS).
about: Spell 125B, the famous 'Negative Confession': the dead person declares innocence before 42 assessor gods, one at a time — 'Hail, [god], who comes forth from [place]: I have not [sin].' All 42 declarations are here, each with hieroglyphs, transliteration, and translation. The hieroglyphs are transcribed from Budge's 1895 plates of the Papyrus of Ani; the transliteration follows the Leiden convention, and the translations are made fresh from the original. Shown left-to-right for reading; on the papyrus the text runs right-to-left.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓅱𓋴𓐍𓈖𓅓𓏏𓂻 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓉺𓊖 𓂜 𓁹 𓀀 𓇋𓋴𓆑𓏏𓅪
sentences.tr: i wsḫ-nmtt pr m iwnw n ir ·i isft
sentences.l: hail · Wide-strider · coming-forth · from · Heliopolis · not · do · I · wrong
sentences.i: Hail, Wide-strider, who comes forth from Heliopolis: I have not done wrong.
sentences.n: The template for every one of the 42 declarations: 'Hail, [god], who comes forth from [place]: I have not [sin].' After a few you will read straight past the frame and just watch the god, the place, and the sin change. wsḫ-nmtt = 'wide of stride'; iwnw is Heliopolis (Budge's 'Annu'); isft, 'wrong / falsehood', is the opposite of maꜥat.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓎛𓊪𓏏𓊮 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓐍𓂋𓂝𓎛𓄿𓊖 𓂜 𓂝𓅱𓄿𓂡 𓀀
sentences.tr: i ḥpt-sšt pr m ḫr-ꜥḥꜣ n ꜥwꜣ ·i
sentences.l: hail · Embracer-of-flame · coming-forth · from · Kher-aha · not · plunder · I
sentences.i: Hail, Embracer-of-flame, who comes forth from Kher-aha: I have not plundered.
sentences.n: ḥpt-sšt = 'embracer of flame' (Budge: 'Hept, embraced with flame'). ḫr-ꜥḥꜣ, Kher-aha, is an old district by the Nile near Heliopolis (Old Cairo). ꜥwꜣ, 'plunder, rob', is the very verb from the first draft — here in its real setting.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓆑𓈖𓍿𓅱𓀭 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓐍𓏇𓏌𓅱𓊖 𓂜 𓍿𓄿𓅱𓂡 𓀀
sentences.tr: i fnṯw pr m ḫmnw n ṯꜣw ·i
sentences.l: hail · Fenti · coming-forth · from · Hermopolis · not · steal · I
sentences.i: Hail, Fenti, who comes forth from Hermopolis: I have not stolen.
sentences.n: fnṯw, 'the Nose' (Budge 'Fenti'), a god named for the nose. ḫmnw is Hermopolis, the 'city of the Eight' primeval gods. ṯꜣw = 'steal, take away'.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓂝𓅓𓈙𓅱𓏏 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓈎𓂋𓏏𓊖 𓂜 𓋴𓅓𓄿𓌪 𓀀 𓂋𓍿𓀀𓁐𓏥
sentences.tr: i ꜥm-ḫꜣibit pr m qrt n smꜣ ·i rmṯ
sentences.l: hail · Eater-of-shades · coming-forth · from · Qernet · not · slay · I · men
sentences.i: Hail, Eater-of-shades, who comes forth from Qernet: I have not slain men.
sentences.n: ꜥm-ḫꜣibit = 'eater of shades' — one of the more sinister assessors. smꜣ = 'slay, kill'; rmṯ = 'people, mankind'. Budge marks the men-sign 'twice', i.e. written to mean people in general.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓈖𓎛𓄿𓁷 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓂋𓄿𓊃𓏏𓄿𓅱𓈉 𓂜 𓎛𓆓𓏏 𓀀 𓂧𓃀𓎛𓏏𓐁
sentences.tr: i nḥꜣ-ḥr pr m rꜣ-sṯꜣw n ḥḏ ·i dbḥ
sentences.l: hail · Dangerous-face · coming-forth · from · Rosetau · not · defraud · I · offerings
sentences.i: Hail, Dangerous-face, who comes forth from Rosetau: I have not defrauded the offerings.
sentences.n: nḥꜣ-ḥr = 'fierce / dangerous of face' (Budge 'Neha-hra'). rꜣ-sṯꜣw, Rosetau, is the necropolis realm of Sokar near Giza. ḥḏ = 'damage, defraud'; dbḥ, the offerings that are due.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓃭𓃭𓏏𓄛 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓊪𓏏𓇯 𓂜 𓐍𓃀𓄿 𓀀
sentences.tr: i rwti pr m pt n ḫbꜣ ·i
sentences.l: hail · Double-Lion · coming-forth · from · sky · not · diminish · I
sentences.i: Hail, Double-Lion, who comes forth from heaven: I have not diminished the oblations.
sentences.n: rwti (Budge 'Rereti'), the 'Double Lion' — the twin lions Shu and Tefnut, guardians of the horizon; here the god comes forth from the sky (pt). ḫbꜣ = 'diminish, reduce'. Budge restores the object '[oblations]' from parallel copies — the sin is stinting the gods' food-offerings.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓁹𓆑 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓊃𓄿𓅱𓏏𓊖 𓂜 𓍿𓄿𓂡 𓀀 𓆱𓏏𓊹
sentences.tr: i mꜣꜣ-f pr m sꜣwt n ṯꜣ ·i ḫt-nṯr
sentences.l: hail · Whose eyes are of fire · coming-forth · from · Asyut · not · despoil · I · god's property
sentences.i: Hail, Whose eyes are of fire, who comes forth from Asyut: I have not despoiled the property of the god.
sentences.n: mꜣꜣ-f — 'Whose eyes are of fire'; from Asyut. The sin: I have not despoiled the property of the god.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓈖𓃀𓄿𓊮 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓐍𓏏𓐍𓏏𓊖 𓂜 𓎼𓂋𓎼𓀁 𓀀
sentences.tr: i nbꜣ pr m ḫtḫt n grg ·i
sentences.l: hail · Flame · coming-forth · from · Khetkhet · not · tell-lies · I
sentences.i: Hail, Flame, who comes forth from Khetkhet: I have not told lies.
sentences.n: nbꜣ — 'Flame'; from Khetkhet. The sin: I have not told lies.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓋴𓍿𓈎𓋴𓅱 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓈖𓈖𓇓𓅱𓊖 𓂜 𓈖𓎛𓅓𓂡 𓀀 𓂝𓈎𓅱𓏒
sentences.tr: i sṯ-qsw pr m nni-nsw n nḥm ·i ꜥqw
sentences.l: hail · Bone breaker · coming-forth · from · Herakleopolis · not · carry-off · I · food
sentences.i: Hail, Bone breaker, who comes forth from Herakleopolis: I have not carried off food.
sentences.n: sṯ-qsw — 'Bone breaker'; from Herakleopolis. The sin: I have not carried off food.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓅱𓏏𓅱𓊮 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓉗𓂓𓊪𓏏𓎛 𓂜 𓎡𓈖𓂡 𓀀
sentences.tr: i wtw-nsr pr m ḥwt-kꜣ-ptḥ n kn ·i
sentences.l: hail · Flame shooter · coming-forth · from · Memphis · not · do-violence · I
sentences.i: Hail, Flame shooter, who comes forth from Memphis: I have not done violence.
sentences.n: wtw-nsr — 'Flame shooter'; from Memphis. The sin: I have not done violence.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓈎𓂋𓏏𓏭 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓋀𓏏𓈉 𓂜 𓈖𓎡𓂺 𓀀
sentences.tr: i qrti pr m imntt n nk ·i
sentences.l: hail · Qerti · coming-forth · from · the West · not · fornicate · I
sentences.i: Hail, Qerti, who comes forth from the West: I have not committed fornication.
sentences.n: qrti — 'Qerti'; from the West. The sin: I have not committed fornication.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓁷𓆑𓄂𓆑 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓏏𓊪𓎛𓏏𓉐𓆑 𓂜 𓋴𓂋𓅓𓁿 𓀀
sentences.tr: i ḥr-f-ḥꜣ-f pr m tpḥt-f n srmi ·i
sentences.l: hail · His face behind him · coming-forth · from · his cavern · not · make-weep · I
sentences.i: Hail, His face behind him, who comes forth from his cavern: I have not made anyone weep.
sentences.n: ḥr-f-ḥꜣ-f — 'His face behind him'; from his cavern. The sin: I have not made anyone weep.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓃀𓄿𓋴𓏏𓆇 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓈙𓏏𓄿𓏏𓉐 𓂜 𓅱𓈖𓅓𓁃 𓀀 𓄣
sentences.tr: i bꜣsti pr m štꜣt n wnm ·i ib
sentences.l: hail · Bast · coming-forth · from · the hidden place · not · devour · I · heart
sentences.i: Hail, Bast, who comes forth from the hidden place: I have not been rapacious (devoured the heart).
sentences.n: bꜣsti — 'Bast'; from the hidden place. The sin: I have not been rapacious (devoured the heart).

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓏏𓄿𓊮𓂾 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓐍𓐍𓅱𓇰 𓂜 𓏏𓎛𓂻 𓀀
sentences.tr: i tꜣ-rd pr m ꜣḫḫ n th ·i
sentences.l: hail · Fiery-legs · coming-forth · from · the darkness · not · transgress · I
sentences.i: Hail, Fiery-legs, who comes forth from the darkness: I have not transgressed.
sentences.n: tꜣ-rd — 'Hot/fiery of legs' (Budge: 'Blazing legs'); comes forth from ꜣḫḫ, 'the darkness' (Budge's Akhekh). th = 'to transgress, overstep'.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓂝𓅓𓋴𓈖𓆑 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓈖𓅓𓅓𓏏𓉐 𓂜 𓁹 𓀀 𓎛𓈖𓅱𓏏
sentences.tr: i ꜥm-snf pr m nmmt n ir ·i hn
sentences.l: hail · Blood-eater · coming-forth · from · the-block · not · commit · I · deceit
sentences.i: Hail, Blood-eater, who comes forth from the block: I have not acted deceitfully.
sentences.n: ꜥm-snf — 'Eater of blood' (Budge: Am-snef); comes forth from nmmt, 'the block / place of slaughter'. The sin: I have not acted deceitfully.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓂝𓅓𓄡𓊃𓎡 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓅓𓄿𓃀𓏏𓊖 𓂜 𓅱𓈙𓂡 𓀀 𓎛𓈖𓃀𓏏𓈅
sentences.tr: i ꜥm-bsk pr m mꜣbt n wš ·i ḥnbt
sentences.l: hail · Eater-of-entrails · coming-forth · from · Mabet · not · lay-waste · I · ploughed-land
sentences.i: Hail, Eater-of-entrails, who comes forth from Mabet: I have not laid waste ploughed land.
sentences.n: ꜥm-bsk — 'Eater of entrails' (Budge: Am-besek); from Mabet. wš = 'lay waste'; ḥnbt, ploughed/cultivated land.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓎟𓐙𓏏𓆄 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓐙𓐙𓏏𓏭𓊖 𓂜 𓅓𓏏𓅓𓏏𓀁 𓀀
sentences.tr: i nb-mꜣꜥt pr m mꜣꜥti n mtmt ·i
sentences.l: hail · Lord of Maat · coming-forth · from · Maati · not · eavesdrop · I
sentences.i: Hail, Lord of Maat, who comes forth from Maati: I have not been an eavesdropper.
sentences.n: nb-mꜣꜥt — 'Lord of Maat'; from Maati. The sin: I have not been an eavesdropper.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓍿𓈖𓅓𓅱𓂾 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓃀𓄿𓋴𓏏𓊖 𓂜 𓈝𓂋 𓀀
sentences.tr: i ṯnmiw pr m bꜣst n šm-r ·i
sentences.l: hail · Backward strider · coming-forth · from · Bubastis · not · set-mouth-going · I
sentences.i: Hail, Backward strider, who comes forth from Bubastis: I have not spoken against anyone in anger.
sentences.n: ṯnmiw — 'Backward strider'; from Bubastis. The sin: I have not spoken against anyone in anger.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓋴𓂋𓏏𓏭𓆙 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓉺𓊖 𓂜 𓐍𓂝𓂋𓀁 𓀀
sentences.tr: i srti pr m iwnw n ḫꜥr ·i
sentences.l: hail · Sertiu · coming-forth · from · Heliopolis · not · be-wroth · I
sentences.i: Hail, Sertiu, who comes forth from Heliopolis: I have not been angry without just cause.
sentences.n: srti — 'Sertiu'; from Heliopolis. The sin: I have not been angry without just cause.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓏏𓅱𓏏𓅱𓆑𓀐 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓄿𓏏𓏭𓊖 𓂜 𓈖𓎡𓂺 𓀀 𓍔𓏏𓁐
sentences.tr: i tuti-f pr m ꜣti n nk ·i ḥmt
sentences.l: hail · Doubly wicked · coming-forth · from · Ati · not · defile · I · a wife
sentences.i: Hail, Doubly wicked, who comes forth from Ati: I have not committed adultery.
sentences.n: tuti-f — 'Doubly wicked'; from Ati. The sin: I have not committed adultery.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓅱𓄿𓅓𓅓𓏏𓆙 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓐍𓃀𓏏𓉐 𓂜 𓈖𓎡𓂺 𓀀 𓍔𓏏𓁐
sentences.tr: i wꜣmmti pr m ẖbt n nk ·i ḥmt
sentences.l: hail · Uamemti · coming-forth · from · the place of execution · not · defile · I · a wife
sentences.i: Hail, Uamemti, who comes forth from the place of execution: I have not lain with another man's wife.
sentences.n: wꜣmmti — 'Uamemti'; from the place of execution. The sin: I have not lain with another man's wife.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓁹𓈖𓏏𓏭𓆑 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓉐𓏠𓈖𓅱𓊖 𓂜 𓏏𓄿𓏏𓄿𓂡 𓀀
sentences.tr: i mꜣꜣ-int-f pr m pr-mnw n tꜣtꜣ ·i
sentences.l: hail · Watcher-of-what-is-brought · coming-forth · from · Panopolis · not · pollute · I
sentences.i: Hail, Watcher-of-what-is-brought-to-him, who comes forth from Panopolis: I have not polluted myself.
sentences.n: mꜣꜣ-int-f — 'Watcher of what is brought to him' (Budge: Maa-antu-f); comes forth from pr-mnw, Panopolis (Akhmim). tꜣtꜣ = 'to pollute, defile (oneself)'.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓁷𓅨 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓄿𓅓𓅓𓏏𓊖 𓂜 𓁹 𓀀 𓉔𓂋𓅱𓀐
sentences.tr: i ḥr-wr pr m ꜣmmt n ir ·i hrw
sentences.l: hail · Chief-of-the-mighty · coming-forth · from · Amemt · not · cause · I · terror
sentences.i: Hail, Chief-of-the-mighty, who comes forth from Amemt: I have not caused terror.
sentences.n: ḥr-wr — 'Chief of the mighty' (Budge: Her-uru); comes forth from Amemt (ꜣmmt). The sin: I have not caused terror (ir hrw).

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓐍𓅓𓇋𓅱𓀭 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓎡𓊃𓇋𓅱𓊖 𓂜 𓏏𓎛𓂻 𓀀
sentences.tr: i ḫmiw pr m ksiw n th ·i
sentences.l: hail · Khemiu · coming-forth · from · Kesiu · not · transgress · I
sentences.i: Hail, Khemiu, who comes forth from Kesiu: I have not transgressed / committed offence.
sentences.n: ḫmiw — 'Khemiu'; from Kesiu. The sin: I have not transgressed / committed offence.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓋴𓈙𓏏𓀁 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓅨𓂋𓏏𓊖 𓂜 𓏏𓄿𓊮 𓀀 𓄣
sentences.tr: i sšt-ḫrw pr m wryt n tꜣ ·i ib
sentences.l: hail · Disposer of speech · coming-forth · from · Urit · not · be-hot · I · of heart
sentences.i: Hail, Disposer of speech, who comes forth from Urit: I have not been hot-tempered.
sentences.n: sšt-ḫrw — 'Disposer of speech'; from Urit. The sin: I have not been hot-tempered.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓐍𓈖𓀔 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓅱𓄿𓃀𓊖 𓂜 𓋴𓐍𓀁 𓀀 𓐙𓏏𓆄
sentences.tr: i nḫnw pr m wꜣb n sḫ ·i mꜣꜥt
sentences.l: hail · Babe · coming-forth · from · Uab · not · be-deaf · I · to truth
sentences.i: Hail, Babe, who comes forth from Uab: I have not turned a deaf ear to the words of truth.
sentences.n: nḫnw — 'Babe'; from Uab. The sin: I have not turned a deaf ear to the words of truth.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓎡𓈖𓅓𓏏𓏭𓀭 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓎡𓈖𓅓𓏏𓊖 𓂜 𓋴𓈖𓈙𓀁 𓀀
sentences.tr: i knmti pr m knmt n snš ·i
sentences.l: hail · Kenemti · coming-forth · from · Kenemet · not · cause-grief · I
sentences.i: Hail, Kenemti, who comes forth from Kenemet: I have not caused grief.
sentences.n: knmti — 'Kenemti'; from Kenemet. The sin: I have not caused grief.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓇋𓈖𓊵𓏏𓊪𓆑 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓊃𓄿𓅱𓊖 𓂜 𓁹 𓀀 𓂝𓆓𓄿𓀐
sentences.tr: i in-ḥtp-f pr m sꜣw n ir ·i ꜥḏꜣ
sentences.l: hail · Bringer of his offering · coming-forth · from · Sais · not · act · I · insolence
sentences.i: Hail, Bringer of his offering, who comes forth from Sais: I have not acted insolently.
sentences.n: in-ḥtp-f — 'Bringer of his offering'; from Sais. The sin: I have not acted insolently.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓋴𓂋𓐍𓂋𓅱𓀁 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓃹𓈖𓊃𓏏𓊖 𓂜 𓋴𓐍𓈖𓂡 𓀀 𓐍𓈖𓈖𓅱𓀐
sentences.tr: i sr-ḫrw pr m wnst n sḫn ·i ḫnnw
sentences.l: hail · Disposer of speech · coming-forth · from · Unaset · not · stir-up · I · strife
sentences.i: Hail, Disposer of speech, who comes forth from Unaset: I have not stirred up strife.
sentences.n: sr-ḫrw — 'Disposer of speech'; from Unaset. The sin: I have not stirred up strife.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓎟𓁷𓏥 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓈖𓆓𓆑𓏏𓊖 𓂜 𓅱𓋴𓍿𓈖𓂻 𓀀
sentences.tr: i nb-ḥrw pr m nṯft n wsṯn ·i
sentences.l: hail · Lord of faces · coming-forth · from · Netchefet · not · judge-hastily · I
sentences.i: Hail, Lord of faces, who comes forth from Netchefet: I have not judged hastily.
sentences.n: nb-ḥrw — 'Lord of faces'; from Netchefet. The sin: I have not judged hastily.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓋴𓐍𓂋𓇋𓅱𓀭 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓅱𓏏𓈖𓊖 𓂜 𓋴𓅓𓏏𓅓𓏏𓀁 𓀀
sentences.tr: i sḫriw pr m wtn n smtmt ·i
sentences.l: hail · Sekheriu · coming-forth · from · Uten · not · eavesdrop · I
sentences.i: Hail, Sekheriu, who comes forth from Uten: I have not been an eavesdropper.
sentences.n: sḫriw — Sekheriu; comes forth from Uten (wtn). smtmt = 'to eavesdrop, pry into others' affairs'.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓎟𓂝𓃀𓅱𓏭 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓊃𓄿𓅱𓏏𓊖 𓂜 𓂝𓈙𓊤𓀁 𓀀
sentences.tr: i nb-ꜥbwi pr m sꜣwti n ꜥš-ḫrw ·i
sentences.l: hail · Lord-of-two-horns · coming-forth · from · Sais · not · be-loud-of-voice · I
sentences.i: Hail, Lord-of-two-horns, who comes forth from Sais: I have not multiplied my words.
sentences.n: nb-ꜥbwi — 'Lord of two horns' (Budge: Neb-abui); from Sais (sꜣwti). ꜥš-ḫrw, literally 'loud of voice' — to talk overmuch.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓄤𓏏𓅓𓀭 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓉗𓂓𓊪𓏏𓎛 𓂜 𓁹 𓀀 𓃀𓇋𓈖𓅪
sentences.tr: i nfr-tm pr m ḥwt-kꜣ-ptḥ n ir ·i bin
sentences.l: hail · Nefertem · coming-forth · from · Memphis · not · do · I · evil
sentences.i: Hail, Nefertem, who comes forth from Memphis: I have not done evil.
sentences.n: nfr-tm — 'Nefertem'; from Memphis. The sin: I have not done evil.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓏏𓅓𓀭𓇳 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓊽𓅱𓊖 𓂜 𓋴𓎛𓅱𓂋𓀁 𓀀 𓇓𓏏𓈖
sentences.tr: i tm-m-trw pr m ḏdw n sḥwr ·i nswt
sentences.l: hail · Tem in his seasons · coming-forth · from · Busiris · not · curse · I · the king
sentences.i: Hail, Tem in his seasons, who comes forth from Busiris: I have not cursed the king.
sentences.n: tm-m-trw — 'Tem in his seasons'; from Busiris. The sin: I have not cursed the king.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓁹𓅓𓄣𓆑 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓏏𓃀𓅱𓊖 𓂜 𓂋𓎛𓈖𓈗 𓀀 𓈗
sentences.tr: i iri-m-ib-f pr m tbw n rḥn ·i mw
sentences.l: hail · Working in his heart · coming-forth · from · Tebu · not · foul · I · water
sentences.i: Hail, Working in his heart, who comes forth from Tebu: I have not fouled water.
sentences.n: iri-m-ib-f — 'Working in his heart'; from Tebu. The sin: I have not fouled water.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓐖𓎛𓇋𓀭 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓏌𓅱𓈗 𓂜 𓋴𓈎𓄿𓂝 𓀀 𓊤
sentences.tr: i ꜥḥi pr m nw n sqꜣ ·i ḫrw
sentences.l: hail · Sistrum bearer · coming-forth · from · Nu · not · make-loud · I · voice
sentences.i: Hail, Sistrum bearer, who comes forth from Nu: I have not spoken arrogantly (raised my voice).
sentences.n: ꜥḥi — 'Sistrum bearer'; from Nu. The sin: I have not spoken arrogantly (raised my voice).

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓎗𓂋𓐍𓇋𓏏𓏥 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓊃𓄿𓅱𓊖 𓂜 𓋴𓎛𓅱𓂋𓀁 𓀀 𓊹
sentences.tr: i wḏ-rḫyt pr m sꜣw n sḥwr ·i nṯr
sentences.l: hail · Provider of mankind · coming-forth · from · Sais · not · curse · I · the god
sentences.i: Hail, Provider of mankind, who comes forth from Sais: I have not cursed the god.
sentences.n: wḏ-rḫyt — 'Commander of mankind' (Budge: Uatch-rekhit); from Sais (sꜣw). The sin: I have not cursed God (sḥwr nṯr).

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓈖𓎛𓃀𓂓𓀭 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓏏𓊪𓎛𓏏𓉐𓆑 𓂜 𓁹 𓀀 𓊃𓄿𓈎𓂡
sentences.tr: i nḥb-kꜣ pr m tpḥt-f n ir ·i sꜣq
sentences.l: hail · Neheb-ka · coming-forth · from · his cavern · not · commit · I · theft
sentences.i: Hail, Neheb-ka, who comes forth from his cavern: I have not committed theft.
sentences.n: nḥb-kꜣ — Neheb-ka; from his cavern (tpḥt-f). The sign is damaged; Budge reads the sin as robbery/theft (sꜣq), with a query.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓈖𓎛𓃀𓄤𓏏𓀭 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓏏𓊪𓎛𓏏𓉐𓆑 𓂜 𓎛𓆓𓏏 𓀀 𓊪𓄿𓅱𓏏𓏒𓊹𓊹𓊹
sentences.tr: i nḥb-nfrt pr m tpḥt-f n ḥḏ ·i pꜣwt-nṯrw
sentences.l: hail · Neheb-nefert · coming-forth · from · his cavern · not · defraud · I · gods'-cakes
sentences.i: Hail, Neheb-nefert, who comes forth from his cavern: I have not defrauded the gods of their cake-offerings.
sentences.n: nḥb-nfrt — Neheb-nefert (paired with Neheb-ka just before); from his cavern (tpḥt-f). ḥḏ pꜣwt-nṯrw = to defraud the gods of their pat-cakes (offering-bread).

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓋴𓐍𓂋𓏏𓁶 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓎡𓂋𓏏𓉐𓆑 𓂜 𓈖𓎛𓅓𓂡 𓀀 𓐍𓈖𓆑𓅱𓏒
sentences.tr: i sšr-tp pr m krt-f n nḥm ·i ḫnfw
sentences.l: hail · Arranger of the head · coming-forth · from · his shrine · not · carry-away · I · cake offerings
sentences.i: Hail, Arranger of the head, who comes forth from his shrine: I have not stolen the khenfu-cakes of the blessed dead.
sentences.n: sšr-tp — 'Arranger of the head'; from his shrine. The sin: I have not stolen the khenfu-cakes of the blessed dead.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓇋𓈖𓂝𓆑 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓐙𓐙𓏏𓏭𓊖 𓂜 𓈖𓎛𓅓𓂡 𓀀 𓂝𓈎𓅱𓏒𓐍𓈖𓀔
sentences.tr: i in-ꜥ-f pr m mꜣꜥti n nḥm ·i ꜥqw-nḫn
sentences.l: hail · Bringer of his arm · coming-forth · from · the twin city of Maat · not · snatch · I · a child's food
sentences.i: Hail, Bringer of his arm, who comes forth from the twin city of Maat: I have not snatched away the food of children.
sentences.n: in-ꜥ-f — 'Bringer of his arm'; from the twin city of Maat. The sin: I have not snatched away the food of children.

sentences.t: 𓇋 𓎛𓆓𓅱𓃀𓎛 𓉐𓂻 𓅓 𓇾𓈙𓈗𓊖 𓂜 𓋴𓐍𓏏𓂡 𓀀 𓊹𓊖𓆑
sentences.tr: i ḥḏ-ꜥbḥw pr m tꜣ-š n sḫt ·i nṯr-niwt-f
sentences.l: hail · White teeth · coming-forth · from · the Lake · not · blaspheme · I · the god of his town
sentences.i: Hail, White teeth, who comes forth from the Lake: I have not blasphemed the god of my city.
sentences.n: ḥḏ-ꜥbḥw — 'White teeth'; from the Lake. The sin: I have not blasphemed the god of my city.

glossary.i: i — O!, hail (the vocative interjection that opens each declaration)
glossary.pr: pr — comes forth, 'who comes forth' (participle of pri, to go/come out)
glossary.iwnw: iwnw — Heliopolis, the sun-cult city (Budge: Annu)
glossary.n: n — not; negates the verb ('I have not …')
glossary.·i: ·i — I, me (1st-person singular suffix pronoun; the seated-man sign)
glossary.isft: isft — wrong, falsehood, disorder — the opposite of maꜥat
glossary.ḥpt-sšt: ḥpt-sšt — 'Embracer of flame', the 2nd assessor god (Budge: Hept-shet)
glossary.ḫr-ꜥḥꜣ: ḫr-ꜥḥꜣ — Kher-aha, a district on the Nile near Heliopolis (Old Cairo)
glossary.fnṯw: fnṯw — 'the Nose', the 3rd assessor god (Budge: Fenti)
glossary.ḫmnw: ḫmnw — Hermopolis, city of the eight primeval gods (Budge: Khemennu)
glossary.ꜥm-ḫꜣibit: ꜥm-ḫꜣibit — 'Eater of shades', the 4th assessor god
glossary.qrt: qrt — Qernet, the place the 4th god comes forth from
glossary.nḥꜣ-ḥr: nḥꜣ-ḥr — 'Dangerous of face', the 5th assessor god (Budge: Neha-hra)
glossary.rꜣ-sṯꜣw: rꜣ-sṯꜣw — Rosetau, the Sokar necropolis realm near Giza
glossary.rwti: rwti — the 'Double Lion' (Shu and Tefnut), the 6th assessor god (Budge: Rereti)
glossary.mꜣꜣ-f: mꜣꜣ-f — 'Whose eyes are of fire', 7th assessor (Budge: Fenti-of-fire)
glossary.ḫt-nṯr: ḫt-nṯr — the property of the god
glossary.grg: grg — to speak falsehood, tell lies
glossary.nḥm: nḥm — to take away, carry off, snatch
glossary.kn: kn — to be violent, do harm
glossary.imntt: imntt — the West, the realm of the dead
glossary.nk: nk — to copulate; (here) fornicate / commit adultery
glossary.srmi: srmi — to make (someone) weep (causative of rmi)
glossary.ẖnt: ẖnt — 'the shambles/place of slaughter' (reading to confirm)
glossary.wš: wš — to lay waste, make desolate
glossary.mꜣꜥti: mꜣꜥti — Maati, the hall/city of the Two Truths
glossary.ṯnmiw: ṯnmiw — 'He who strides backward', 18th assessor (Budge: Tenemiu)
glossary.šm-r: šm-r — 'to set the mouth going' — to speak against, babble
glossary.mꜣꜣ-int-f: mꜣꜣ-int-f — 'Watcher of what is brought to him', the 22nd assessor god (Budge: Maa-antu-f)
glossary.sšm: sšm — to cause terror, to lead astray
glossary.wsṯn: wsṯn — to act/judge with undue haste
glossary.sḫꜣ: sḫꜣ — to pry into, be inquisitive
glossary.nb-ꜣbwi: nb-ꜣbwi — 'Lord of horns', 32nd assessor (reading to confirm)
glossary.sqꜣ: sqꜣ — to make high/loud (causative of qꜣi)
glossary.wḏ-rḫyt: wḏ-rḫyt — 'Commander of mankind', 37th assessor (reading to confirm)
glossary.pꜣwt-nṯrw: pꜣwt-nṯrw — the cake-offerings of the gods
glossary.ꜥqw-nḫn: ꜥqw-nḫn — the food of a child
glossary.nṯr-niwt-f: nṯr-niwt-f — the god of his town
glossary.tꜣ-rd: tꜣ-rd — 'Fiery of legs' (Budge: 'Blazing legs'), the 14th assessor god
glossary.ꜣḫḫ: ꜣḫḫ — 'the darkness' (Budge: Akhekh), the place the 14th god comes forth from
glossary.ꜥm-snf: ꜥm-snf — 'Eater of blood', the 15th assessor god (Budge: Am-snef)
glossary.nmmt: nmmt — 'the block, the place of slaughter/execution'
glossary.ḥr-wr: ḥr-wr — 'Chief of the mighty' (Budge: Her-uru), the 23rd assessor god
glossary.ꜣmmt: ꜣmmt — Amemt, the place the 23rd god comes forth from
glossary.wtn: wtn — Uten, the place the 31st god comes forth from
glossary.nb-ꜥbwi: nb-ꜥbwi — 'Lord of two horns', the 32nd assessor god (Budge: Neb-abui)
glossary.ꜥš-ḫrw: ꜥš-ḫrw — 'loud of voice'; to multiply one's words, talk overmuch
glossary.sꜣq: sꜣq — robbery, theft (Budge reads the damaged sin here with a query)
